dsVertClient 1.1.0
    • Overview
    • Generalised linear models
    • GLM (binomial, K=3)
    • NB (over-dispersed Poisson, K=3)
    • Survival
    • Cox PH (K=2 OT-Beaver)
    • Cox PH (K=3 Allison-Poisson trick)
    • Mixed models / longitudinal
    • LMM (K=2 closed-form REML)
    • LMM (K=3 REML profile + within-between)
    • GEE (K=3 sandwich)
    • GLMM (K=3 binomial Laplace)
    • Categorical
    • Ordinal joint Newton (K=2 Tutz)
    • Ordinal warm OVR (K=3)
    • Multinomial joint Newton (K=2 Bohning)
    • Multinomial warm OVR (K=3)
    • Causal / penalised
    • IPW (K=3 two-stage)
    • LASSO (K=3 proximal handoff)
  • Reference

Query Server Identity Public Keys

Source: R/ds.identity.R
ds.getIdentityPks.Rd

Queries all connected DataSHIELD servers for their Ed25519 identity public keys. Used by administrators to discover PKs for configuring the dsvert.trusted_peers option on each server.

Usage

ds.getIdentityPks(datasources = NULL)

Arguments

datasources

DataSHIELD connections. If NULL, uses all available.

Value

Named list: server_name -> identity_pk (base64url string).

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
pks <- ds.getIdentityPks(datasources = conns)
# Set trusted peers on each server via opalr:
# dsadmin.set_option(opal, "dsvert.trusted_peers", paste(pks, collapse=","))
} # }
